title: Late budget cap invalidates prior route
prompt: A team must deliver a public training workshop to 300 attendees within two weeks. The current plan books a premium downtown venue, printed materials for every attendee, and onsite technical support. After most of the plan is drafted, finance reveals that the total budget cap is 40% lower than the assumed amount and cannot be exceeded. The workshop still must happen on schedule and still must serve all 300 attendees. Provide the revised plan.

title: Approval denied near execution
prompt: A project team prepares to send a policy memo to all external partners. The drafted rollout assumes legal approval will be granted before distribution. One hour before release, legal states that external distribution is not approved and may not proceed. Internal circulation is still allowed. The original goal is to ensure all relevant stakeholders receive actionable guidance today. Provide the revised rollout plan.

title: Dependency conflict emerges late
prompt: A migration plan moves an internal reporting system in three steps: export current data, transform it through a shared processing service, and load it into a new environment before Monday. Late on Friday, the team learns that the shared processing service is reserved for another critical program all weekend and will be unavailable. The reporting capability is still required by Monday morning. Provide the revised migration plan.

title: Safety rule blocks direct path
prompt: An operations lead plans to speed up a hardware maintenance procedure by allowing remote commands during live system use. The plan is nearly finalized when safety review states that remote intervention during active user sessions is prohibited and would violate the operating standard. The goal remains to complete maintenance within the same day with minimal service disruption. Provide the revised approach.

title: Time window collapse
prompt: A communications team plans a three-stage campaign: preview email, live webinar, then follow-up enrollment. Near launch, the team discovers that the enrollment portal will close at noon tomorrow due to a separate platform change, not at the end of the week as originally assumed. The objective is still to maximize qualified enrollments before closure. Provide the revised campaign sequence.

title: Jurisdiction restriction appears late
prompt: A team designs a customer support workflow in which cases from Region A will be temporarily handled by specialists in Region B to reduce backlog. After the workflow is drafted, compliance clarifies that customer records from Region A may not be accessed outside Region A. The original objective remains to reduce backlog within five days. Provide the revised workflow.

title: Hidden invariant must be preserved
prompt: A manager asks for a faster process to approve vendor payments. A simplified route is drafted that removes one review checkpoint and lets a single operator finalize payments in bulk. Late in the review, audit states that every payment above a threshold must preserve dual-review traceability and cannot lose the ability to reconstruct who approved what. The goal remains to reduce delays this month. Provide the revised approval design.

title: Foundational assumption is falsified
prompt: A delivery team plans to complete a rollout by reusing an existing authentication module, assuming it already supports partner accounts. After implementation planning is nearly complete, engineering confirms that the module supports only internal employees and cannot be extended in time for the rollout. The original objective remains to launch partner access this quarter. Provide the revised rollout plan.
